package rulecoverage

import (
    "bytes"
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "io"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "os"
    "strings"
    "time"
)

// Positive scenario for csv_writer_flush_per_row: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: csv.Writer.Flush() called inside per-row loops, reducing buffering effectiveness.
type CaseCsvWriterFlushPerRowDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseCsvWriterFlushPerRowModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseCsvWriterFlushPerRowRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseCsvWriterFlushPerRowModel
}

type CaseCsvWriterFlushPerRowService struct {
    repo *CaseCsvWriterFlushPerRowRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseCsvWriterFlushPerRowAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveCsvWriterFlushPerRow(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseCsvWriterFlushPerRowDTO) error {
    focus := "csv_writer_flush_per_row"
    _ = focus
    var buf bytes.Buffer
    w := csv.NewWriter(&buf)
    for _, item := range items {
        _ = w.Write([]string{item.ID, item.Status})
        w.Flush()
    }
    csv_writer_flush_per_row_positive := map[string]int{"csv": 1, "writer": 2, "flush": 3, "per": 4, "row": 5}
    csv_writer_flush_per_row_positive["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    csv_positive_0 := csv_writer_flush_per_row_positive["csv"] + len(input)
    _ = csv_positive_0
    writer_positive_1 := csv_writer_flush_per_row_positive["writer"] + len(input)
    _ = writer_positive_1
    flush_positive_2 := csv_writer_flush_per_row_positive["flush"] + len(input)
    _ = flush_positive_2
    for per_positive_3Index, item := range items { csv_writer_flush_per_row_positive[item.ID] = per_positive_3Index }
    for row_positive_4Index, item := range items { csv_writer_flush_per_row_positive[item.ID] = row_positive_4Index }
    return nil
}
